worked.claim: The rooftop garden lowers the library's afternoon roof temperature on clear days.

items.text: The garden contains herbs, flowers, and two benches.
items.why: It is about the garden but gives no temperature comparison.

items.text: At 15:00 on six clear days, paired sensors under the planted and bare sections recorded the planted section as cooler each day.
items.why: It measures the named outcome in a paired comparison; it does not by itself establish every season or every roof.

items.text: A visitor said the whole library felt cooler after the garden opened.
items.why: It is not a controlled roof-temperature comparison and mixes indoor perception with the claim.

items.text: On a seventh clear day, the planted sensor was warmer after its shade cover detached.
items.why: It bears on the outcome, but the changed condition limits comparison.

readings.label: Topic match
readings.text: An item counts as evidence when it is about the same subject as the claim.

readings.label: Credibility relation
readings.text: An item counts as evidence when its result bears on what the claim says happened.

compiler.disagreement: Does a detail about the garden count when it contains no temperature outcome or comparison?

support.text: Which card contains the outcome named in the claim?
Point to the claim, outcome, and comparison columns before deciding.
Read the same two cards as a claim, evidence, and relation table.

rules.label: Evidence must bear on the exact outcome named by the claim.

transfer.claim: The new morning bus route reduced late arrivals at Northbridge Learning Centre during the trial.

items.text: The trial buses were blue and displayed route maps.
items.why: It shares the topic but does not bear on late arrivals.

items.text: The centre logged 31 late arrivals in the four weeks before the trial and 12 in the four trial weeks, using the same start time and attendance rule.
items.why: It compares the named outcome across matched windows; other changes remain possible.

items.text: The trial occurred during exam month, when attendance rules also changed.
items.why: It identifies a change that could affect the outcome and limits the simple attribution.

results.demonstrated: On this unfamiliar authored bus-route task, the learner independently met the named claim-to-evidence classification criteria once.
results.notDemonstrated: On this unfamiliar authored bus-route task, the submitted selections did not yet meet the named claim-to-evidence classification criteria.
remainsUntested: Whether this single bus-route classification transfers to other claims or contexts.
Whether the learner can evaluate real-world truth, source authenticity, bias, statistical significance, or causal attribution.
Whether the learner can write, revise, or evaluate persuasive arguments.
Whether the distinction is retained over time or across repeated unaided attempts.
Whether the authored fixture and its access routes are valid for representative learners.
Whether a local, honour-based, non-persisted receipt establishes durable or independent evidence.
